pattern: Build a trie from the word list, then DFS from every board cell following only trie branches to prune impossible paths early and collect all reachable words.
hints: Searching each word independently repeats a lot of work; insert all words into a trie so a single DFS pass from each cell explores all matching words simultaneously.
During DFS, advance a trie pointer with each step and mark a word as found when you reach an end-of-word node; mark cells visited during recursion to avoid reuse.
Prune the trie as words are found to avoid revisiting completed prefixes, and remove leaf trie nodes that led only to already-collected words.

edge_inputs.description: classic 4x4 board with two findable words
word would require reusing a cell — not found
single cell board, no word matches
word list contains duplicates in name but board finds none
all board cells the same letter and word uses only that letter
multiple words share a prefix found on the board
word longer than total board cells cannot exist

stress.description: small board over tiny alphabet so many word paths exist and trie pruning is exercised
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    m = min(size, 12)
    n = min(size, 12)
    alpha = 'abcde'
    board = [[rng.choice(alpha) for _ in range(n)] for _ in range(m)]
    words = set()
    for _ in range(200):
        length = rng.randint(1, min(8, m * n))
        words.add(''.join(rng.choice(alpha) for _ in range(length)))
    return [board, list(words)]
stress.note: tiny alphabet increases hit rate; oracle is brute-force per word so stays manageable
